content:
9 min read
Just now

--

There is a strange moment that happens when you use AI coding agents for real work.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

At first, everything feels magical. You ask a question, it writes code. You paste an error, it fixes it. You describe a feature, it gives you a full plan with files, functions, and even test cases. For some time, you start feeling like coding has changed forever.
Then reality comes.
The AI coding agent makes one small wrong assumption. Then another. Then it edits a file without understanding why that logic was written in that ugly way. Then it explains the mistake so confidently that you almost believe it.
That was exactly what was happening with me.
My AI coding agent was not useless. Actually, it was very helpful. But it was guessing too much. It guessed my folder structure. It guessed what my old Python files were doing. It guessed where a helper function should be. It guessed which part of the code was important and which one was outdated.
And the most dangerous part was this: it never looked unsure.
It gave every answer like it had seen the full project, even when I knew it had only seen one small file.
